title: creochiton anomala
intro: Creochiton anomala, also known as the Australian sea spurrey, is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. This aquatic plant has been traditionally used in Australian folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Creochiton anomala are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and treat various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or ointment for topical application, and its leaves can be dried and used as a tea to harness its medicinal properties.
